title: Hokas Tokas — pity gift to an unarmed beggar
prompt: Hokas notices the empty hands and the wreck of a man before him. He buries the worry behind his usual gruff manner, disappears behind the bar, and comes back with a worn short sword in a plain scabbard. He sets it on the bar with a solid thunk. "I don't need it anymore. Don't tell me what you do with it. Don't bring it back."

choices.label: Accept the sword (publicly receive charity)
choices.resolutionHint: Hero takes the blade. Hokas pretends not to notice the moment of weight. Standing erodes a touch — the room saw — but the soul receives the kindness.

choices.label: Refuse the sword (preserve dignity)
choices.resolutionHint: Hero walks out unarmed. Hokas watches the door for a long moment after.
